mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Excessively high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 280) triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 280 despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust demand conditions that drive corporate earnings optimism, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint growth of GDPC1 and HOUST fails to produce positive 60d returns in > 70% of cases, or their correlation drops below 0.4.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 4% signals tight labor markets that increase wage pressures and corporate costs, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE falls below 4% despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates improving supply-side capacity utilization, which reduces inflationary pressures and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to produce positive 5d returns in > 70% of cases, or the signal's IC drops below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signal weakening demand conditions that depress corporate revenues, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce negative 20d returns in > 70% of cases, or their correlation drops below -0.3.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 150k signals a resilient labor market that boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in < 40% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 150k, or the signal's IC drops below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 4.5% triggers recession risks that depress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% despite the signal.
